findings.description: Stale docstring in `SdkHarnessAdapter.thread_id` (src/assistant/harnesses/base.py). The docstring incorrectly states that `DeepAgentsHarness` sets its `thread_id` in `create_agent`. Following the round-1 fix for gemini #5, the ID is now set during `__init__` to ensure instance-lifetime stability.
findings.resolution: Update the docstring in `src/assistant/harnesses/base.py` to reflect that `DeepAgentsHarness` synthesizes its `thread_id` at construction time.

findings.description: Stale docstring in `DeepAgentsHarness.astream_invoke` (src/assistant/harnesses/sdk/deep_agents.py). The docstring claims that tool-call lifecycle correlation happens "without extra bookkeeping". The round-1 fix for gemini #2 introduced the `open_tool_calls` dictionary to provide this bookkeeping.
findings.resolution: Update the docstring in `src/assistant/harnesses/sdk/deep_agents.py` to reflect the use of `open_tool_calls` for stable ID correlation.

findings.description: Missing `aclosing` for upstream SDK streams in harness implementations. While the `traced_harness` decorator (decorators.py) now uses `aclosing` on the harness generator, the harness methods themselves (`deep_agents.py` and `ms_agent_fw.py`) do not use `aclosing` when consuming SDK-provided streams (`agent.astream_events` and `agent.run(stream=True)`). If the harness generator is closed mid-stream (e.g., client disconnect), the upstream SDK iterators may remain un-finalized until garbage collection.
findings.resolution: Import `aclosing` from `contextlib` in both `src/assistant/harnesses/sdk/deep_agents.py` and `src/assistant/harnesses/sdk/ms_agent_fw.py`, and wrap the upstream SDK stream consumption in `async with aclosing(...) as stream:` blocks.
